text: Scheme Name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) Description: A central sector scheme to provide income support to all landholder farmer families across the country, subject to certain exclusions, to supplement their financial needs for procuring various inputs related to agriculture and

their name Benefits: 6,000 per year in three equal installments of 2,000 each, transferred directly to the bank accounts of eligible farmer families. Required Documents: Aadhaar card Land records land documents Bank account details Residence proof Identity proof (Voter ID Driving License)

ce proof Identity proof (Voter ID Driving License) Application Process: Register on PM-KISAN portal (pmkisan.gov.in) 2. Fill the application form with personal and land details 3. Upload required documents 4. Submit application for verification 5. Application verified by state authorities 6.

Scheme Name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY) Description: Housing for All initiative aimed at providing affordable housing to the urban poor by 2022. The mission addresses housing shortage through credit linked subsidy, affordable housing in partnership, and subsidy for beneficiary-led individual

ership, and subsidy for beneficiary-led individual house construction. Eligibility Criteria: Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) Low Income Groups (LIG) Middle Income Groups (MIG) Annual family income up to 18,00,000 for MIG-II Should not own a pucca house anywhere in India Benefits: Interest

a pucca house anywhere in India Benefits: Interest subsidy of up to 6.50 on home loans for 20 years, reducing EMI burden. Maximum subsidy of 2.67 lakh for MIG-II category. Required Documents: Aadhaar card PAN card Income certificate Address proof Bank account details Photographs No-own-house

roof Bank account details Photographs No-own-house declaration Application Process: Apply through Common Service Centers or online portal 2. Submit application with required documents 3. Verification by concerned authorities 4. Sanction of subsidy based on eligibility 5.
ies 4. Sanction of subsidy based on eligibility 5. Disbursement through bank account Category: housing State: All India
Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY)

Housing for All initiative aimed at providing affordable housing to the urban poor by 2022. The mission addresses housing shortage through credit linked subsidy, affordable housing in partnership, and subsidy for beneficiary-led individual house construction.
Interest subsidy of up to 6.50 on home loans for 20 years, reducing EMI burden. Maximum subsidy of 2.67 lakh for MIG-II category.

Apply through Common Service Centers or online portal 2. Submit application with required documents 3. Verification by concerned authorities 4. Sanction of subsidy based on eligibility 5. Disbursement through bank account
Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) | Low Income Groups (LIG) | Middle Income Groups (MIG) | Annual family income up to 18,00,000 for MIG-II | Should not own a pucca house anywhere in India
Aadhaar card | PAN card | Income certificate | Address proof | Bank account details | Photographs | No-own-house declaration

Scheme Name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) Description: Centralized platform for various scholarship schemes offered by central and state governments for students belonging to economically weaker sections, minority communities, and other deserving categories.

ority communities, and other deserving categories. Eligibility Criteria: Students from class 1 to post-graduation Minimum 50 marks in previous examination Annual family income less than 2,00,000 for SC ST Annual family income less than 1,00,000 for OBC Should be studying in recognized institution

amination Income certificate Caste certificate (if applicable) Bank account details Student ID proof Residence certificate Application Process: Register on National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in) 2. Select relevant scholarship scheme 3. Fill application form with academic details 4.

3. Fill application form with academic details 4. Upload required documents 5. Submit application for verification 6. Verification by institute and state authorities 7. Scholarship amount disbursed to bank account Category: education State: All India

Scheme Name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) Description: A scheme aimed at providing LPG connections to women from BPL households to reduce health hazards caused by cooking with fossil fuels and to prevent indoor air pollution.

fossil fuels and to prevent indoor air pollution. Eligibility Criteria: Adult woman (18 years or above) Belongs to BPL family Has a valid Aadhaar card No existing LPG connection in household Must have a bank account Benefits: Free LPG connection with stove and first refill. Security deposit waived.

h stove and first refill. Security deposit waived. Interest-free loan for stove cost. Required Documents: Aadhaar card of applicant BPL certificate or ration card Bank account details Address proof Photographs No LPG connection declaration Application Process: Visit nearest LPG distributor 2.

lication Process: Visit nearest LPG distributor 2. Fill PMUY application form 3. Submit required documents 4. Verification by distributor 5. LPG connection provided within 7-10 days 6. First refill delivered after connection Category: energy State: All India
Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
A scheme aimed at providing LPG connections to women from BPL households to reduce health hazards caused by cooking with fossil fuels and to prevent indoor air pollution.
Free LPG connection with stove and first refill. Security deposit waived. Interest-free loan for stove cost.

Visit nearest LPG distributor 2. Fill PMUY application form 3. Submit required documents 4. Verification by distributor 5. LPG connection provided within 7-10 days 6. First refill delivered after connection

Scheme Name: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY) Description: National Health Protection Scheme that aims to provide health coverage to poor and vulnerable families. It provides cashless hospitalization coverage of 5,00,000 per family per year.

text: Scheme Name: Skill India Mission - Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) Description: Flagship scheme for skill development of youth to enable them to take up industry-relevant skill training that will help them in securing a better livelihood.

text: Scheme Name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) Description: A government-backed savings scheme for the girl child to encourage parents to build a fund for their daughter s education and marriage expenses. Eligibility Criteria: Girl child below 10 years of age Maximum two girl children per family

years of age Maximum two girl children per family Indian resident Parents can open account on behalf of girl child Valid for natural and adopted girl children Benefits: High interest rate (currently 7.6 per annum). Tax benefits under Section 80C. Minimum deposit of 250 per year.

nder Section 80C. Minimum deposit of 250 per year. Maximum deposit of 1,50,000 per year. Required Documents: Birth certificate of girl child Aadhaar card of guardian PAN card of guardian Address proof of guardian Photographs Bank account details Application Process: Visit authorized bank or post

Application Process: Visit authorized bank or post office 2. Fill SSY account opening form 3. Submit required documents 4. Deposit initial amount 5. Account opened with passbook 6. Regular deposits can be made anytime Category: financial_inclusion State: All India
Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
A government-backed savings scheme for the girl child to encourage parents to build a fund for their daughter s education and marriage expenses.
High interest rate (currently 7.6 per annum). Tax benefits under Section 80C. Minimum deposit of 250 per year. Maximum deposit of 1,50,000 per year.
Visit authorized bank or post office 2. Fill SSY account opening form 3. Submit required documents 4. Deposit initial amount 5. Account opened with passbook 6. Regular deposits can be made anytime
Girl child below 10 years of age | Maximum two girl children per family | Indian resident | Parents can open account on behalf of girl child | Valid for natural and adopted girl children
Birth certificate of girl child | Aadhaar card of guardian | PAN card of guardian | Address proof of guardian | Photographs | Bank account details

Scheme Name: Mahila Coir Yojana Description: A scheme to promote self-employment among rural women by providing them with training and financial assistance for setting up coir-based units. Eligibility Criteria: Women aged 18-55 years Rural area residents Below poverty line or from weaker sections

sidents Below poverty line or from weaker sections Willing to undergo training No regular employment Benefits: Free training in coir processing. 90 subsidy for equipment cost. Working capital assistance. Marketing support.

st. Working capital assistance. Marketing support. Required Documents: Aadhaar card Age proof Income certificate Residence proof Bank account details Photographs BPL certificate (if applicable) Application Process: Apply through Coir Board office 2. Submit application with documents 3.

ard office 2. Submit application with documents 3. Selection for training program 4. Complete 2-month training 5. Submit project report for unit setup 6. Receive subsidy and assistance Category: women_empowerment State: Coconut producing states
Mahila Coir Yojana
A scheme to promote self-employment among rural women by providing them with training and financial assistance for setting up coir-based units.
Free training in coir processing. 90 subsidy for equipment cost. Working capital assistance. Marketing support.

Apply through Coir Board office 2. Submit application with documents 3. Selection for training program 4. Complete 2-month training 5. Submit project report for unit setup 6. Receive subsidy and assistance

Aadhaar card | Age proof | Income certificate | Residence proof | Bank account details | Photographs | BPL certificate (if applicable)
